response:
the area of the regular hexagonal community center with each side measuring thirty meters is calculated by dividing the shape into six equilateral triangles and using the formula involving the square of the side length multiplied by the square root of three divided by two then multiplied by six which results in approximately two thousand six hundred fifty point eight three square meters for the total floor space available for the new facility.

the radius of the circular garden touching the midpoints of the hexagon sides corresponds to the apothem of the hexagon which is found by multiplying the side length by the square root of three divided by two yielding a radius of approximately twenty point five nine meters and using this value the area of the circle is computed by squaring the radius and multiplying by pi to get roughly one thousand six hundred sixty point two eight square meters for the central green space.
